name: Tamale Pie 1911
recipeIngredient: 10 dried red chiles (such as New Mexico or California)
3 tablespoons meat drippings; lard or shortening
recipeInstructions:
MEAT: Combine beef chuck, pork shoulder, bay leaf, onion, cloves, peppercorns, and salt to taste in a large pot with water to cover. Cook over low heat until tender, about 2 1/2 hours. Remove beef and pork from the pot. Strain stock and reserve 2 1/2 cups. Discard bay leaf, onion, cloves, and peppercorns. Cut meat into 1-inch cubes.
CHILE GRAVY: Cut stem ends off chiles and soak overnight in cold water to cover. Remove seeds and veins and simmer in water to cover for 30 minutes. Scrape pulp out of skins. Puree the chile pulp and set aside.
Melt lard in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir in flour and salt and cook over low heat, stirring until thickened, about 3 to 4 minutes. Add 2 cups reserved stock and simmer, stirring, for another 3 to 4 minutes. Stir in chile puree and remove from heat.
CRUST: Combine remaining 1/2 cup reserved meat stock, cornmeal, egg, and lard until thickened.
ASSEMBLY: Sprinkle raisins evenly in the bottom of a 1 1/2-quart baking dish. Sprinkle olives over. Place meat cubes on top of the olives and pour chile gravy over. Spoon the cornmeal crust mixture evenly over the top. Bake at 325 degrees until the crust is golden brown, about 1 hour.
[6 servings. Each serving: 562 calories; 1,715 mg sodium; 130 mg cholesterol; 33 grams fat; 34 grams carbohydrates; 35 grams protein; 2.18 grams fiber.]
